summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #658 in the moltis repo — a tool-call reflex loop where the agent repeatedly calls tools with empty/invalid arguments for up to 25 iterations before hitting max_iterations, wasting tokens and never recovering.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented, tested, and merged via PR #664: a pre-dispatch schema validator (tool_arg_validator.rs), a two-stage ring-buffer loop detector (tool_loop_detector.rs), wired into both streaming and non-streaming runner paths, plus config fields and 24+ new unit/integration tests. PR review comments were also addressed in a follow-up commit.

repo: The AgentTool trait exposes parameters_schema() in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:15, making runner-level validation possible without touching individual tools.
runner.rs has two parallel dispatch loops (non-streaming and streaming) that must be kept in sync for any cross-cutting runner change.
Loop state is tracked via fields like malformed_retry_count and auto_continue_count — the loop detector was added alongside these.
crates/chat/src/lib.rs contains a RunnerEvent forwarder that requires explicit handling of every RunnerEvent variant — new variants must be added there.
Config changes require touching schema.rs (struct fields + defaults), validate.rs (build_schema_map entries), and template.rs (TOML template).
CLAUDE.md specifies: no changelog entry, conventional commit format, nightly-2025-11-30 toolchain for fmt/clippy.
The bd (Beads) database was offline for this worktree — requires bd dolt pull or bd-worktree-attach.sh to fix.
just lint and just test are blocked by a pre-existing CUDA toolkit issue in llama-cpp-sys-2, unrelated to agents changes.
Taplo reports pre-existing TOML format issues in files not touched by this change.

code.finding: tool_context injection happens before validation — context-injected keys can satisfy required fields, so validation must run after context injection.
ToolCallStart was originally emitted before tool execution; moved to after schema validation so rejected calls never show a spurious 'Executing...' UI state.
Existing mock tools and MockProvider pattern used for runner integration tests — ReflexExecProvider and LegitimateRetryProvider followed the same pattern.
Bug found in review: clear_strip_tools() must also clear the ring buffer, otherwise the detector can oscillate (stage-2 forced-text turn re-triggers stage-1 on the very next iteration).
Bug found in review: integer JSON values (Value::Number where is_i64/is_u64) must be accepted when schema type is 'number', not just floats.

workflow: Greptile automated review caught two real bugs (oscillation in loop detector, integer-vs-float type check) that were missed in initial implementation — worth treating P2 Greptile findings seriously.
Adding a 4-tuple return from tool dispatch futures (success, result, error_msg, rejected_flag) was cleaner than trying to signal rejection via the error string alone.
Running cargo test -p <crate> is much faster than cargo test --workspace for iterating on a single crate.
Format fixes should be applied before the final commit to avoid a separate format-only commit.

friction: bd (Beads issue tracker) database was offline for the worktree — could not file a tracking issue as planned.
just lint and just test are broken in this environment due to a pre-existing CUDA/llama-cpp-sys-2 dependency issue, requiring manual per-crate cargo commands as a workaround.
Taplo reports pre-existing TOML formatting violations in untouched files, making it impossible to get a clean taplo check without fixing unrelated files.
Rate limit hit mid-session ('You've hit your limit · resets 8pm') requiring user to resume the session.
The streaming path dispatch block required multiple read-then-edit cycles to get the exact old_string correct for the Edit tool.
open_items: bd worktree database is offline — needs bd dolt pull or ./scripts/bd-worktree-attach.sh before issue tracking works in this worktree.
just lint / just test remain broken due to CUDA toolkit absence — pre-existing, but should be resolved for full CI parity locally.
Taplo TOML formatting violations in 5 pre-existing files were not fixed as they are out of scope for this PR.
The tool_arg_validator is deliberately minimal (catches missing-required and primitive type mismatches only) — a full JSON Schema validator could be added later if needed.
No e2e/browser test was added for the new ToolCallRejected and LoopInterventionFired events in the activity log UI (crates/web/src/assets/chat.js).
